name: High-Bandwidth Brain-Computer Interfaces
what: Implanted electrode arrays that read large numbers of neurons to let people with paralysis control cursors, robotic limbs, or speech synthesisers.
how: Thin electrode threads record cortical activity; on-board chips decode intended movement in real time and stream it wirelessly to devices.

name: Consumer EEG & fNIRS Wearables
what: Non-invasive headbands that estimate brain and attention states for consumer wellness, meditation, and sleep-staging applications.
how: Dry electrodes and near-infrared optodes sense electrical and blood-oxygen signals, which on-device models translate into simple state metrics.

name: Adaptive Deep Brain Stimulation
what: Closed-loop implants that sense pathological brain rhythms and adjust stimulation in real time, improving symptom control and battery life.
how: Sensing electrodes detect biomarker oscillations and a pulse generator modulates stimulation only when symptoms are detected.

name: Neuroinflammation Therapeutics
what: Drugs targeting microglial and immune pathways in the brain to slow the progression of Alzheimer's and other neurodegenerative conditions.
how: Small molecules and antibodies modulate inflammatory signalling and clearance of misfolded proteins, aiming to protect neurons.

name: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)
what: Enables direct communication between the brain and external devices.
how: Uses electrodes to read neural signals and translate them into actions.

name: Neurostimulation Techniques for Pain Management
what: Uses electrical or magnetic stimulation to modulate neural activity.
how: Provides non-invasive treatments for chronic pain and other neurological disorders.

name: Neural Prosthetics
use: Replacing or enhancing human neural functions with advanced devices.
what: Creating implantable devices that can interface directly with the brain to restore or augment sensory and motor functions.
how: Using microelectrodes and biocompatible materials to integrate these devices seamlessly into the nervous system.

name: High-Density CMOS Probes
what: Arrays of thousands of electrodes integrated onto a single silicon shank.
how: Using CMOS fabrication to embed amplifiers and digitizers directly at the electrode site.

name: High-Density BCI
what: Thousands of electrode channels for high-bandwidth neural data.
how: Thin-film flexible polymer arrays implanted in the motor cortex.

name: Functional MRI (fMRI)
what: Non-invasive imaging technique for brain activity.
how: Enhance fMRI resolution and speed.

name: Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS)
what: Targeted electrical stimulation for disease management.
how: Develop more precise and effective DBS techniques.

name: Neural Quantum Computing
what: Combines quantum computing with brain-computer interfaces for ultra-fast processing.
how: Quantum qubits are integrated into neural networks to enhance computational power.

name: Neural Prosthetics for Consciousness Transfer
what: Neural prosthetics enable the transfer of consciousness from one brain to another or into a digital environment.
how: By mapping and decoding neural signals, this technology can create a digital replica of human consciousness.

name: Neuralink's Brain-Computer Interfaces
what: Advanced brain-computer interfaces for treating neurological disorders and enhancing cognitive functions.
how: Using advanced neural implants to interface with the human brain.

name: Neural Uploads
what: Neural uploads involve the transfer of a human mind into a digital format. This could enable the possibility of living forever in a virtual environment or preserving one's consciousness for future generations.
how: By mapping and replicating neural connections and patterns, these systems can create an exact digital copy of a person’s mind.

name: Whole Brain Emulation
what: The process of scanning a physical brain at the synaptic level and simulating it on hardware.
how: High-resolution connectome mapping combined with neuromorphic computing substrates.

name: Neural Implants for Pain Management
what: Provide long-term, non-invasive pain relief solutions.
how: Develop targeted delivery systems and biocompatible materials.

name: Neural Prosthetics for Consciousness
what: Advanced neural prosthetics can interface directly with the brain to restore or enhance consciousness and cognitive functions.
how: By integrating quantum sensors and processors, these devices can provide unprecedented levels of brain-computer interaction.

name: Digital Mind Uploading
what: Digital mind uploading involves transferring a human's consciousness into a computer or artificial body.
how: This process uses advanced neuroimaging and AI to map the brain's neural patterns and recreate them in a virtual environment.

name: Neural Quantum Interface
what: A quantum-based BCI could enable unprecedented levels of brain-machine integration.
how: Uses quantum entanglement to create highly efficient and responsive neural interfaces.
